facts: Kinetic energy is the energy of motion; a rolling ball has kinetic energy because it is moving, while a stationary ball has none.
Potential energy is stored energy; a ball held above the ground has gravitational potential energy because of its height, and it transforms into kinetic energy when dropped.
Thermal energy transfers from a warmer object to a cooler object; a metal spoon in hot soup gets warm because thermal energy moves from the soup to the spoon.
In a collision, energy is transferred between objects; when a moving toy car hits a stationary one, the stationary car starts moving because it gained kinetic energy from the collision.
Engineers use energy transformation to solve problems; a solar-powered calculator transforms light energy into electrical energy to work without batteries.
misconceptions: Students think energy is a substance that can be used up and disappear, rather than understanding that energy transfers or transforms but is not lost.
Students believe that only moving objects have energy and that a stationary object has no energy at all, ignoring stored potential energy.
